output_one_label: Which circuit option has final OUT value 1?
Select the circuit whose final OUT node evaluates to 1.
Evaluate each circuit. Which option's final OUT value is 1?
Which circuit panel produces a final output of 1?
output_zero_label: Which circuit option has final OUT value 0?
Select the circuit whose final OUT node evaluates to 0.
Evaluate each circuit. Which option's final OUT value is 0?
Which circuit panel produces a final output of 0?
assignment_outputs_one_label: Which option assignment makes the source circuit output 1?
Select the assignment option that produces final OUT value 1.
Using the shown x, y, and z values, which option makes the circuit evaluate to 1?
Which labeled assignment sets the source circuit's final OUT value to 1?
Find the option row whose input values make OUT equal 1.
assignment_outputs_zero_label: Which option assignment makes the source circuit output 0?
Select the assignment option that produces final OUT value 0.
Using the shown x, y, and z values, which option makes the circuit evaluate to 0?
Which labeled assignment sets the source circuit's final OUT value to 0?
Find the option row whose input values make OUT equal 0.
gate_type_count: How many {target_gate_type} gate symbols are in the circuit?
How many visible gates use the {target_gate_type} symbol?
In the circuit, how many gate symbols represent {target_gate_type}?
Count every {target_gate_type} gate shown in the circuit.
internal_output_one_count: Using the input values shown, how many gates output 1?
Evaluate each gate. How many gate symbols have output value 1?
With the shown input values, count the gates whose output is 1.
How many visible gates evaluate to 1?
internal_output_zero_count: Using the input values shown, how many gates output 0?
Evaluate each gate. How many gate symbols have output value 0?
With the shown input values, count the gates whose output is 0.
How many visible gates evaluate to 0?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_clean_worksheet: a clean worksheet-style Boolean logic-gate circuit diagram with labeled inputs, standard gate symbols, wires, and final OUT nodes
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_notebook_problem: a notebook-style Boolean logic-gate circuit diagram with labeled inputs, standard gate symbols, wires, and final OUT nodes
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_exam_scan: an exam-scan-style Boolean logic-gate circuit diagram with labeled inputs, standard gate symbols, wires, and final OUT nodes
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_output_one_label: set "annotation" to the pixel-space bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] of the selected circuit panel whose final OUT value is 1
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_output_one_label: set "answer" to the single capital-letter circuit option label
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_output_zero_label: set "annotation" to the pixel-space bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] of the selected circuit panel whose final OUT value is 0
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_output_zero_label: set "answer" to the single capital-letter circuit option label
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_assignment_outputs_one_l: set "annotation" to an object with keys "source_circuit" and "selected_option", each mapped to a pixel-space bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1]
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_assignment_outputs_one_label: set "answer" to the single capital-letter option label
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_assignment_outputs_zero_: set "annotation" to an object with keys "source_circuit" and "selected_option", each mapped to a pixel-space bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1]
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_assignment_outputs_zero_labe: set "answer" to the single capital-letter option label
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_gate_type_count: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space gate-symbol bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1], one for each counted gate; use [] if no gate matches
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_gate_type_count: set "answer" to the integer number of matching gates
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_internal_output_one_coun: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space gate bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1], one for each gate whose computed output is 1; use [] if no gate matches
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_internal_output_one_count: set "answer" to the integer number of gates whose computed output is 1
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_internal_output_zero_cou: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space gate bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1], one for each gate whose computed output is 0; use [] if no gate matches
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_internal_output_zero_count: set "answer" to the integer number of gates whose computed output is 0
